tasks.label: Graded assignment — 90% done, submit when finished

memory.summary: With only 10% left and no other immediate demands, I'll finish this assignment to protect my team's trust rather than rushing for a perfect grade.
I'm at 92% with an hour left, so I'll hold my pace to finish strong rather than rushing. My team's trust matters more than a perfect grade right now.
The deadline just passed while I was at 94%, failing to submit on time. This directly undermines the team trust I value so highly, and now I must face the consequences.
I've finally pushed the assignment to 99% completion, but submitting late after missing the deadline still risks the team trust I prioritize. I need to address this breach directly with my teammates.

desires: Directly confront my teammates to renegotiate the terms of our collaboration and restore the trust I just breached by missing the deadline.
Secure a concrete, long-term plan with my team that prevents future submission failures rather than simply apologizing for this specific error.
Protect my physical stamina and sleep discipline tonight to ensure I can handle the emotional weight of this conflict without burning out.

goals.description: Submit the Graded assignment immediately (99% complete) to stop the bleeding of trust before the system locks out.
goals.rationale: My deliberate nature and high sleep discipline mean I cannot afford to let this task linger. Submitting now is the only way to demonstrate reliability and open the door for the honest conversation about the failure that follows. Every minute spent polishing the final 1% is a delay in owning the situation, which erodes my core value of relationships.

goals.description: Initiate a direct, text-based message to teammate_A and professor_assignment acknowledging the miss, outlining the cause without excuses, and proposing a specific time to renegotiate terms.
goals.rationale: Relationships are my core value (0.5). My persona requires me to set boundaries and renegotiate directly; passive waiting or making excuses would erode the trust with cohort_3 and my teammates further. This must happen once g1 is submitted to ensure I am not reacting from panic but from a place of deliberate accountability.

goals.rationale: My long planning horizon means I cannot afford to have this critical conversation while stressed or reactive. Mitigating stress now ensures I can be clear-headed and assertive tomorrow, preserving my physical capacity for the social work required to repair these bonds.
